content: Weighted-layer aggregation as logistic regression: each layer emits a normalized sub-score x_i in [0,1]; combined score = sigmoid(sum(w_i*x_i) + b). Verdict = score > threshold. Tuning ('backpropagation'): treat human-labeled spam/false-positive pubkeys as labels, fit w_i,b by gradient descent / IRLS on the logistic loss. Standardize/normalize features first. Class imbalance (spam rare) => weight classes or use precision-oriented threshold. Start with hand-set weights (interpretable), then re-fit from labels. Keeps layers independently tunable while giving a principled combiner. Cheap: linear model, no LLM.
